title: Uncontrolled Components – Letting the DOM Handle State

meta.title: React Uncontrolled Components Guide – Using Refs & DefaultValues (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to use uncontrolled components in React. Discover when to use refs instead of state, handling file uploads, and integrating with non-React code.

content.value: In an uncontrolled component, the form data is handled by the DOM itself, rather than by a React component's state. Instead of writing an event handler for every state update, you use a **ref** to pull the values from the DOM when you need them (e.g., when the form is submitted).
To create an uncontrolled component, you use the `useRef` hook to create a reference to the DOM element and access its `.value` property directly.

In the React rendering lifecycle, the `value` attribute on form elements overrides the value in the DOM. With uncontrolled components, you often want React to specify the initial value, but leave subsequent updates uncontrolled. For this, use `defaultValue` instead of `value`.

value: **File Inputs**: The `<input type="file">` is always uncontrolled in React because its value is read-only from a script perspective.
**Simple Forms**: When you don't need instant validation or custom UI feedback while the user types.
**Non-React Integration**: When integrating with third-party DOM libraries (like jQuery plugins) that manage their own internal state.
**Performance**: In extremely large forms where state updates on every keystroke might cause lag (though `useMemo` or React Hook Form are usually better solutions here).

faq.question: Can I use both in the same form?
faq.answer: Yes, it is common to have a controlled text input for validation while having an uncontrolled file input for uploads.

faq.question: Is it 'bad' to use uncontrolled components?
faq.answer: Not at all. While controlled components are the 'React way,' uncontrolled components are perfectly valid for simple use cases or specific constraints like file inputs.

faq.question: How do I clear an uncontrolled input?
faq.answer: You must manually manipulate the DOM: `inputRef.current.value = ''`.

quiz.question: Which prop is used to set the initial value of an uncontrolled input?
What is the primary tool used to access uncontrolled input values?
Which type of input is ALWAYS uncontrolled in React?
